3.happening: Count the {len(values)} array slots that describe the tree.
3.why: We need the size before building node objects.
3.invariant: n is the length of the description array.

4.happening: Start an empty list of real tree nodes.
4.why: We build node objects the visualization can draw.
4.invariant: nodes holds every non-null value as a node.

5.happening: Start a lookup from node id to node object.
5.why: BFS fetches nodes by id in O(1).
5.invariant: by_id[i] is the node stored at slot i.

6.why: It records each node as unseen, queued, or visited — that drives the colors.

7.happening: Loop over every slot to initialize its state.
7.invariant: Slots below i already have a state.

9.happening: Start the child-slot walker just past the root.
9.why: Children are handed out in array order from slot 1.
9.invariant: child is the next unassigned slot.

10.happening: Loop over every slot to build the nodes.
10.why: Each real value becomes a node linked to its children.
10.invariant: Slots below i are already built.

11.why: A null slot is a placeholder, not a node.

12.happening: Create node {i} with value {values[i]} and empty child links.
12.why: Children are filled in from the following slots.
12.invariant: A fresh node starts with no children.

13.happening: Is there a slot left to be the left child?
13.why: Children come from the next slots in order.
13.invariant: child points at the candidate left child.

14.happening: Is that candidate slot a real node (not null)?
14.invariant: A null candidate leaves the left child empty.

15.happening: Link slot {child} as node {i}'s left child.
15.why: It is the next real node in level order.
15.invariant: left now points at a real child id.

16.why: Each slot is claimed exactly once.

17.happening: Is there a slot left to be the right child?
17.why: The right child is the slot after the left.
17.invariant: child points at the candidate right child.

18.happening: Is that candidate slot a real node (not null)?
18.invariant: A null candidate leaves the right child empty.

19.happening: Link slot {child} as node {i}'s right child.
19.why: It is the next real node in level order.
19.invariant: right now points at a real child id.

20.why: Each slot is claimed exactly once.

21.happening: Add the finished node to the tree.
21.why: It now has its value and child links.
21.invariant: nodes grows by one built node.

22.happening: Register node {i} under its id for fast lookup.
22.invariant: by_id maps every id to its node.

23.invariant: Nodes are processed front to back.

24.happening: No node is being visited yet (cur = -1).
24.why: The cursor is hidden until the sweep starts.

25.happening: The tree is non-empty — seed the queue with the root.
25.why: Level order starts at the root.

25.happening: The tree is empty — nothing to sweep.
25.why: An empty tree inverts to itself.

26.happening: Enqueue the root id (0) to begin.
26.why: The root is the first node inverted.

27.why: Queued nodes are amber, waiting to be visited.
27.invariant: A node enters the queue at most once.

28.happening: The queue holds nodes — invert the next one.
28.why: BFS processes nodes in the order they were queued.
28.invariant: Every reachable node passes through the queue.

28.happening: The queue is empty — every node has been inverted.
28.invariant: The whole tree is now mirrored.

29.happening: Dequeue the front node into cur.
29.why: BFS visits the earliest-queued node next.

30.why: It is being processed now and turns blue.
30.invariant: Visited nodes are never processed again.

31.why: We need the old left before overwriting it.
31.invariant: left holds the pre-swap left child.

32.why: We need the old right before overwriting it.
32.invariant: right holds the pre-swap right child.

33.happening: Set node {cur}'s left to its old right child.
33.why: This is half of swapping the two children.
33.invariant: The left link now points where right did.

34.happening: Set node {cur}'s right to its old left child.
34.why: The two children are now swapped — this node is mirrored.
34.invariant: This node's subtrees have traded sides.

35.happening: Does node {cur} have a left child now?
35.why: Real children join the queue for the next level.

36.happening: Enqueue node {cur}'s left child for the next level.
36.why: It will be inverted one level deeper.

37.invariant: Each node enters the queue once.

38.happening: Does node {cur} have a right child now?
38.why: The right child joins the queue after the left.

39.happening: Enqueue node {cur}'s right child for the next level.
39.why: It will be inverted one level deeper.
39.invariant: Right follows left in the queue.

40.invariant: Each node enters the queue once.

41.happening: The queue is empty — clear the current-node cursor.
41.why: Inversion is complete; nothing is being visited now.

42.why: We serialize the inverted tree to return it.
42.invariant: result will hold the answer array.

43.happening: Start a queue to walk the inverted tree.
43.why: A breadth-first walk emits nodes in level order.

44.happening: The tree is non-empty — seed the walk with the root.
44.why: Level order starts at the root.

44.happening: The tree is empty — nothing to serialize.
44.why: An empty tree serializes to an empty array.

45.happening: Enqueue the root id to begin the walk.
45.invariant: The root leads the level-order output.

46.happening: Ids remain — emit the next one.
46.why: We serialize until the walk queue empties.
46.invariant: Every reachable node is emitted once.

46.happening: The walk queue is empty — serialization is done.
46.invariant: result now holds every node in level order.

47.happening: Dequeue the next id from the front.
47.why: Breadth-first emits the earliest-queued id next.

48.happening: This slot is a missing child.
48.why: Level order records holes as null.
48.invariant: Missing children still take a slot.

48.happening: This id points at a real node.
48.invariant: A real id has a value and two child links.

49.happening: Emit null for the missing child.
49.why: The level-order format keeps holes explicit.

51.happening: Emit this node's value into the result.
51.why: Level order lists values top-down, left-to-right.

52.happening: Queue this node's left child to emit next.
52.why: Children follow their parent in level order.

53.happening: Queue this node's right child after the left.
53.why: Right follows left within a level.

54.happening: The output ends in a null — trim it.
54.why: Level order omits trailing null holes.
54.invariant: No trailing nulls remain in the answer.

54.happening: No trailing null to trim — the answer is clean.
54.why: The array already ends in a real value (or is empty).
54.invariant: result is the final level-order form.

55.why: Trailing holes are not part of the level-order answer.

56.happening: Return the inverted tree in level-order form: {result}.
56.why: Every node's children have been swapped and serialized.

init.happening: Building the tree and seeding the queue with the root.
init.why: We need node objects and links before inverting.
init.invariant: Nodes pop in and connect to their parents.

loop.happening: Sweeping the tree level by level with the queue.
loop.why: BFS inverts every node at a depth before going deeper.
loop.invariant: Each queue snapshot is one level.

move.happening: Dequeuing the front node to invert next.
move.why: BFS visits nodes in the order they were queued.

check.happening: Testing whether a real child exists.
check.why: Only real children are enqueued for the next level.

update.happening: Swapping children and queuing them for the next level.
update.why: Inverting a node is swapping its two child links.
update.invariant: Visited nodes stay visited; children queue in order.

return.happening: Returning the inverted tree in level-order form.
return.why: Every node has been swapped and serialized.
